agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A new 'auto-split' feature is proposed that would automatically divide shipments exceeding 500 kg into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different carrier, bypassing supervisor involvement. This directly conflicts with two established production invariants in the existing domain model.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for physically transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept — a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment. Does not yet exist in the domain model."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The binding of exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once set, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role with authority to reassign Carriers on locked Shipments."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic process that divides an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments with independent Carrier assignments."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is sent out; the system guarantees exactly one Carrier is assigned at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. If a Shipment exists without exactly one Carrier, or with multiple Carriers, the system is in a corrupt state — accountability, tracking, billing, and liability are all undefined.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in the system, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization integrity constraint. It prevents unauthorized mid-flight changes that would break chain-of-custody, accountability, and contractual obligations. Bypassing it corrupts the trust model.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment operation without a Supervisor credential must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment has exactly one Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the dispatch-gate invariant. Dispatching without a single accountable Carrier means no one is responsible for the goods in transit — a physical and legal impossibility in real logistics.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation must fail if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A 501 kg shipment is auto-split into two sub-shipments, but the original Shipment already had a locked Carrier assignment. Who authorizes the reassignment?","expected_behavior":"Cannot be resolved without violating BR-2. The auto-split would implicitly reassign or remove a Carrier from the original Shipment without Supervisor involvement."},{"scenario":"A sub-shipment is created and assigned to a new Carrier, but the parent Shipment entity still exists. Does the parent now have zero Carriers?","expected_behavior":"Violates BR-1. The parent Shipment would either have zero Carriers (corrupt) or must be closed/voided, which is a new lifecycle state not in the model."},{"scenario":"Auto-split creates 3 sub-shipments. One sub-shipment's Carrier fails. Who is accountable for the original Shipment?","expected_behavior":"Accountability is fractured. The 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant exists precisely to ensure a single point of accountability. Multi-carrier splits destroy this."},{"scenario":"Customer queries tracking for their original shipment and finds it has been split into 3 sub-shipments they never agreed to.","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The customer contracted for one shipment. Splitting changes the nature of the service without customer consent."},{"scenario":"The 500 kg threshold is just barely exceeded (e.g., 501 kg). Rounding, re-weighing, or measurement error could cause flip-flopping between split and no-split.","expected_behavior":"Needs hysteresis or tolerance band to prevent unstable behavior at the boundary."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments changes billing, insurance, and liability models. A single shipment billed to one carrier becomes multiple shipments billed to multiple carriers. Contracts, SLAs, and pricing structures may not support this.","operational":"Introduces significant operational complexity: coordinating multiple carriers for what was one shipment, handling partial delivery failures, reconsolidation at destination, and tracking fragmentation.","user":"Customers expect one shipment = one tracking number = one point of contact. Auto-splitting fractures this experience and creates confusion around delivery status, accountability, and claims.","risk":"HIGH. This feature, as specified, directly violates two production invariants (BR-1 and BR-2). Shipping it as described would corrupt domain integrity. Additionally, logistics regulations often require a single responsible party per consignment — splitting may have compliance implications.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must now coordinate multi-carrier logistics for split shipments — fundamentally different workflow."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Their single shipment becomes multiple sub-shipments without consent. Tracking, claims, and accountability are fragmented."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"Now share responsibility for what was a single consignment. Liability boundaries are unclear."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their authority over Carrier reassignment is being bypassed by an automated process, undermining the control model."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Billing model assumes 1 Shipment : 1 Carrier. Multi-carrier splits require new invoicing, reconciliation, and SLA structures."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Chain-of-custody, insurance, and consignment regulations typically assume a single responsible carrier per shipment."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction with two established production invariants. The auto-split feature as described cannot be implemented without violating BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment) and BR-2 (Supervisor-only reassignment). Until these contradictions are resolved — either by evolving the domain model or constraining the feature — no valid acceptance criteria can be defined.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradictions between the feature request and existing invariants are clear and unambiguous","The 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier relationship is a hard invariant, not a soft policy","Supervisor-only reassignment is an authorization invariant, not a preference"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to formally evolve the domain model or is unaware of the conflict","Whether 'sub-shipment' is meant to be a new aggregate root or a child entity of Shipment","Whether regulatory constraints apply to the splitting of consignments in this jurisdiction"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model and its invariants are intentional and load-bearing, not accidental","The product team has not yet proposed changes to the underlying domain model to accommodate this feature"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly violates BR-1 (a Shipment must have exactly one Carrier). Splitting a Shipment across multiple Carriers means either the parent Shipment has zero Carriers (violating BR-1) or sub-shipments are a new entity type not yet in the domain model. The feature cannot be implemented within the current model without breaking this invariant.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Customers","Carriers","Finance"],"options":["A: Introduce a new 'ShipmentGroup' or 'Consignment' aggregate that contains multiple sub-shipments, each preserving the 1:1 Carrier invariant. The parent becomes a logical grouping, not a Shipment.","B: Redefine the Shipment aggregate to support 1:N Carrier relationships, which is a fundamental domain model change requiring migration and audit of all downstream systems.","C: Reject auto-split as proposed. Instead, require Supervisor approval for splits (preserving BR-2) and model sub-shipments as first-class Shipments."],"recommendation":"Option A. Introduce a 'Consignment' aggregate that groups multiple Shipments. Each sub-shipment remains a proper Shipment with exactly one Carrier, preserving BR-1 and BR-3. The Consignment provides the customer-facing tracking unity. This is the standard logistics pattern for multi-carrier freight."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature explicitly bypasses Supervisor involvement for what is effectively a Carrier reassignment operation. The existing model requires Supervisor authorization for any change to Carrier assignment (BR-2). Automating this without Supervisor involvement breaks the authorization model.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Operations","Compliance"],"options":["A: Require Supervisor approval for auto-split (defeats the 'auto' purpose but preserves the invariant).","B: Create a new authorization rule: system-initiated splits due to weight threshold are a distinct operation from manual reassignment, and do not require Supervisor approval.","C: Implement auto-split as a Supervisor-delegated standing rule — Supervisors pre-approve the split policy, so authorization is granted in advance rather than per-event."],"recommendation":"Option C. Supervisors pre-approve the auto-split policy (e.g., 'all shipments over 500 kg may be automatically split'). This preserves the principle that Supervisor authority governs Carrier assignment while enabling automation. The Supervisor delegates, not abdicates."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple Carriers to what is logically one Shipment, breaking the 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses Supervisor involvement for Carrier assignment changes, breaking the authorization invariant.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Once a Carrier is assigned, only a Supervisor can reassign."}]}
